channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Government doing 'everything in our power' to keep SA safe from notorious paedophile
item.description: The South Australian government has filed two applications to either ensure notorious paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy is indefinitely detained or the subject of an extended supervision order.
item.dc:creator: Evelyn Leckie

media:group.media:description: Notorious paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy.

item.title: MND has been called 'the beast'. We still don't know what causes it
item.description: As another rugby league player is diagnosed with motor neurone disease, we asked experts why so little is known about what causes it.
item.dc:creator: Hanan Dervisevic

media:group.media:description: Rabbitohs player Jai Arrow revealed news of his motor neurone disease diagnosis on Wednesday.

item.title: After losing four relatives to dementia, woman shares family's story
item.description: Gwen Knox, from Western Australia, has written a play inspired by her family that she hopes starts important conversations about dementia and truth-telling.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Jackson

media:group.media:description: The immersive play invites audience members to eat scones along with the cast.

item.title: Israeli minister's taunting of activists no surprise to those following his career
item.description: Benjamin Netanyahu has condemned a video of his national security minister taunting activists, including Australians, who have been detained. But it's the prime minister who allowed this behaviour to percolate.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Doran

media:group.media:description: Itamar Ben-Gvir's video has prompted backlash from foreign governments, and his own.

item.title: Angus Taylor spruiks gas extraction as SA Liberals oppose fracking bid
item.description: After telling an Adelaide oil and gas conference "we need to get busy digging and drilling", Opposition Leader Angus Taylor bats away questions about the SA Liberal Party's resistance to fracking in the state's South East.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Kelsall

media:group.media:description: Angus Taylor speaks to the media at the Australian Energy Producers conference in Adelaide.

item.title: Peak body slams ministers' failure to attend Aboriginal housing talks
item.description: The Northern Territory's peak body for Aboriginal housing says it's "disgraceful" ministers from both levels of government have failed to attend a conference on the housing crisis.
item.dc:creator: Felicity James

media:group.media:description: Tom Leeming presents at the NT Aboriginal Housing and Homelands conference.

item.title: Palasics cold case killer previously jailed over million-dollar cigarette plot
item.description: New details have emerged about the violent 1999 home invasion that claimed Irma Palasics's life, including a separate million-dollar cigarette conspiracy that put one of her killers behind bars a decade later.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Byrne

media:group.media:description: Irma Palasics was killed, and her husband Gregor Palasics was beaten during a home invasion in 1999.

item.title: Rundown Rockhampton hotel sells for millions at court-ordered auction
item.description: After its closure in 2014, the formerly popular hotel became dilapidated and was frequented by squatters. It has now sold at a court-ordered auction for $5.75 million.
item.dc:creator: Katrina Beavan and Vanessa Jarrett

media:group.media:description: The property at 161 George Street, Rockhampton City, sold at auction today.

item.title: Woman accused of hit-and-run murder to stand trial
item.description: Larissa Rita Mae-Leigh Sant is accused of planning to kill her then-partner's ex-girlfriend, Kiesha Thompson, before striking her with a car while she was walking.
item.dc:creator: Tara Cassidy

media:group.media:description: CCTV shows the car moments before the alleged hit-and-run murder.

item.title: Driver in critical condition after school bus hit tree in Western Sydney
item.description: All students have been taken off the bus and were assessed by paramedics, Fire and Rescue New South Wales said.
item.dc:creator: Freya Noble

media:group.media:description: Authorities said none of the students were seriously injured in the crash.

item.title: Man faces 29 charges after police allegedly seize guns and antisemitic material
item.description: A man has been refused bail after NSW Police allegedly seized antisemitic material, firearms and explosives from a home on the Central Coast.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Forster

media:group.media:description: A Green Point home has been raided by NSW Police and Border Force

item.title: 'Grossly intoxicated' lawyer's fatal hit-run crash jail term reduced on appeal
item.description: An Adelaide lawyer who drank 1.1 litres of wine before killing a pedestrian in a fatal hit-run crash has won a reduced sentence on appeal — in part because she has a young child.
item.dc:creator: Jordanna Schriever

media:group.media:description: Lauren Jean Willgoose has won a reduced jail term on appeal, but has lost an appeal against her convictions over a fatal hit-run crash.

item.title: Kylie Minogue reveals 'secret' cancer and IVF struggles in Netflix doco
item.description: "Our Kylie" gets candid in the three-part Netflix docuseries, grieving over IVF struggles, Michael Hutchence, and revealing she was diagnosed with cancer a second time in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Al Newstead

media:group.media:description: Kylie Minogue offers candid perspectives and big revelations about her life and career in new Netflix docuseries.

item.title: Australia backs landmark UN climate change ruling as others try to block it
item.description: Australia joins 140 other countries in passing a major United Nations resolution backing a landmark legal ruling on climate change, despite efforts by the United States, Russia and Saudi Arabia to sink it.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Dziedzic

media:group.media:description: Pacific nations banded together to push for the initial ruling at the International Court of Justice last year.

item.title: Worst diphtheria outbreak on record spurs $7m federal vaccine response
item.description: The federal government has stepped in to address the largest diphtheria outbreak since records began, unveiling a multi-million dollar package for more vaccines and workers to deliver them.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Dalzell and Paige Cockburn

media:group.media:description: The government has unveiled $7.2 million toward more vaccines and health workers to deliver them.

item.title: Images show last-known movements of alleged New Town murder victim
item.description: Police release images showing the last known public movements of a Hobart woman who was found dead in her home almost two months later.

media:group.media:description: The last-known movements of the woman was on March 18, police say.

item.title: Why Raúl Castro has been charged now over a plane downing 30 years ago
item.description: The United States has indicted former Cuban president Raúl Castro on murder charges stemming from 1996, reviving one of the lowest points in its tangled history with Cuba.
item.dc:creator: Annabel Bowles

media:group.media:description: Raul Castro has been indicted on murder charges in the United States.

item.title: McDonald's circles Castlemaine but can expect a fight over its 'soul'
item.description: McDonald's says it has identified a site in Castlemaine and is exploring the possibility of establishing a restaurant, but it's not being welcomed by some in the town.
item.dc:creator: Anna McGuinness and Tyrone Dalton

media:group.media:description: Fast food giant McDonald's says it has identified a potential site in Castlemaine.

item.title: Farm worker 'felt sorry' for pensioner who wanted his leg amputated, court hears
item.description: A jury hears a recording of John Yalu explaining to police that he panicked after he amputated the leg of a 66-year-old man he then realised was dying. The 41-year-old is standing trial for murder.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Testa

media:group.media:description: John Yalu, 41, has had a Bislama interpreter by his side during the trial.

item.title: Major staff cuts on the horizon as Football Australia posts record loss
item.description: About 20 per cent of Football Australia staffers face the axe as soccer's governing body posts record losses that doubled last financial year's numbers.

media:group.media:description: Football Australia CEO Martin Kugeler said more than 20 per cent of his workforce is facing the axe.

item.title: Earthquakes hit near regional Queensland town twice in four days
item.description: Experts say this week's seismic activity is unusual, but the region has a pattern of large and clustered earthquakes.
item.dc:creator: Nikki Sorbello

media:group.media:description: Two earthquakes have been recorded near Monto in the same week.

item.title: Five reasons Stephen Colbert is one of the US's most important satirists
item.description: The best satirists do more than entertain. They influence public discourse and leave lasting marks on political life.

media:group.media:description: Late Show host Stephen Colbert is leaving the late-night desk.

item.title: Australia's economy has 'already entered a downturn', economist confirms
item.description: The unemployment rate rose by 0.2 percentage points to 4.5 per cent in April, up from 4.3 per cent in March, according to the Bureau of Statistics.
item.dc:creator: David Taylor

item.title: Relief package on way for NSW communities hit by highway closure
item.description: The NSW government announces $3.5 million for communities that have lost visitors and revenue after part of the Great Western Highway was unexpectedly closed.
item.dc:creator: Xanthe Gregory and Lani Oataway

media:group.media:description: Thousands of cars are being detoured through Lithgow each day, via the Chifley Rd, Darling Causeway and Bells Line.

item.title: Queensland Olympics minister referred to AFP over voting registration
item.description: The Australian Electoral Commission (AEC) confirmed on Thursday it had made the referral.

media:group.media:description: Queensland Sports Minister Tim Mander has been referred to the AFP amid questions about his voting registration.
